summary: First-pass testing of Geek Cards Deck Manager. Backend: 15/15 pytest passed (auth register/login/me, natures, cards CRUD + validation + user isolation, decks CRUD + analysis + warnings, upload to Emergent object storage incl. file retrieval). Frontend: login with admin/admin123 works, dashboard renders 'Admin' + stats tiles, card builder creates a card (name+nature Anjo+stats), card appears in Biblioteca with success toast, Meus Decks page loads, protected route /dashboard redirects to /login after clearing auth.

issues: React hydration warning: <span> rendered inside <option>. Likely caused by emergent instrumentation wrapping text in option; if app-controlled, keep option children as plain text to avoid console noise.

action_items: (Optional) Suppress/avoid <span> inside <option> in CardBuilderPage.jsx rarity select to remove React hydration console warning.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py set_auth_cookie uses secure=True + samesite=None - correct for cross-site preview domain.
bcrypt hashing used properly ($2b$). JWT HS256 with 7-day exp. get_current_user supports cookie + Bearer fallback.
MongoDB _id consistently excluded via projections. user_id scoping enforced on all card/deck queries (good isolation - verified by test).

CORS allow_origins='*' with allow_credentials=True is technically invalid per spec; browsers may reject credentialed requests. Preview works via cookie+Bearer fallback, but recommend setting explicit origin list for production.
No brute-force lockout on /api/auth/login (playbook expects 5-fail lockout). Not implemented; flag for main agent if required.
test_credentials: admin@geekcards.com / admin123 (seeded). New users can register via /api/auth/register.
seed_data_creation: Test-scoped cards/decks created during pytest are cleaned up. One UITEST_* card remains in admin library from frontend test (harmless).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All core APIs + UI flows verified green. Re-use /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py for regression. Known non-blocking items: (1) <span> inside <option> React warning, (2) CORS '*' with credentials, (3) no login lockout. Upload to Emergent object storage works (verified tiny PNG upload + GET /api/files/{path} download).
